message:
perf(cli/blocked): short-circuit JSON/TOON output when storage proves no rows possible

Continuation of the perf series ending at 37b02661 / 08073dcc / e1f9c1d3
that's been chipping the cold-start cost out of br read-only commands.
The blocked command's machine-readable paths (JSON, TOON) still ran the
full local-blockers + external-deps + project-config pipeline before
returning what is, on most projects, an empty array.

Added SqliteStorage::may_have_blocked_command_results() — a deliberately
narrow probe answering "is it possible the blocked query returns >0
rows?" via a single 3-EXISTS SELECT against:

  1) blocked_issues_cache (any cached local blocker)
  2) dependencies (external blocker via depends_on_id, INDEXED BY
     idx_dependencies_depends_on, type IN ('blocks',
     'conditional-blocks', 'waits-for'))
  3) dependencies (external parent-child via issue_id, INDEXED BY
     idx_dependencies_issue, type = 'parent-child')

If the cache is fresh (`!blocked_cache_marked_stale()`) and all three
EXISTS evaluate to 0, we know the full query will yield zero rows, so
the JSON/TOON path emits an empty result without loading config or
walking external deps. Any error from the probe falls back to the slow
path with a debug-level trace, so this can never silently mis-report.

Refactored the existing JSON/TOON match arms behind
`output_structured_blocked()` so the fast path and the slow path share
one renderer (avoids drift if either output format gains stats handling
later).

Also added INDEXED BY hints to the existing has_external_dependencies()
queries that the probe shares structure with — same external-deps
range scan, same index, same row shape. Without the hint, SQLite
sometimes picks a worse plan when the table is small enough that index
selectivity is below the planner's threshold but the hot path runs
millions of times.

Three new tests cover:
- Empty in-memory storage → probe returns false (fast path).
- Local blocker dependency present → probe returns true (slow path).
- External blocker dependency present → probe returns true (slow path).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
